2606.30442.title: The FIL Hypothesis: Inductive Biases Help with Kernel Engineering
2606.30442.paper_summary: The Bitter Lesson, which posits that general-purpose methods that scale with computation and data ultimately outperform those with built-in human knowledge, has become a dominant paradigm in the era of Large Language Models. We revisit this principle by observing a new and critical scaling dimension: the duration of the Feedback Information Loop (FIL), the time required for a system to receive a verification signal after generating a prediction. Most historic successes in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have benefited from near instantaneous feedback (e.g., games or classification tasks), but we argue that future AI applications in science and the physical world will inherently involve FILs ranging from hours to weeks. This trend poses a fundamental scaling limit, as obtaining enough verification steps required by purely data-driven methods becomes practically impossible. Additionally, we propose a method that is orthogonal to purely data-driven approaches, based on human-inspired expert knowledge. The method relies on inductive biases and constraining the solution space. We provide an initial validation of the hypothesis and the method, by studying the real-world GPU programming task, a domain with non-trivial FIL, and demonstrate that incorporating inductive biases yields superior performance over data-driven approaches. The code is released under: https://github.com/ai-nikolai/robust_kernelbench
2606.30442.paper_summary_zh: 苦澀的教訓認為，隨著計算和數據擴展的通用方法最終會超越那些內建人類知識的方法，這已成為大型語言模型時代的主導範式。<lb/>我們通過觀察一個新的關鍵擴展維度來重新審視這一原則：反饋信息循環（FIL）的持續時間，即系統在生成預測後接收驗證信號所需的時間。<lb/>大多數人工智慧（AI）的歷史成功都受益於幾乎瞬時的反饋（例如，遊戲或分類任務），但我們認為未來在科學和物理世界中的AI應用必然會涉及持續時間從幾小時到幾週的FIL。<lb/>這一趨勢提出了一個根本的擴展限制，因為獲得純數據驅動方法所需的足夠驗證步驟在實際上變得不可能。<lb/>此外，我們提出了一種與純數據驅動方法正交的方法，基於人類啟發的專家知識。<lb/>該方法依賴於歸納偏見並限制解決方案空間。<lb/>我們通過研究現實世界的GPU編程任務，這是一個具有非平凡FIL的領域，對假設和方法進行了初步驗證，並展示了納入歸納偏見的性能優於數據驅動方法。<lb/>代碼已發布於：https://github.com/ai-nikolai/robust_kernelbench
2606.30442.author: Nikolai Rozanov et.al.
2606.30442.authors: Nikolai Rozanov, Subhabrata Dutta, Preslav Nakov, Iryna Gurevych
